title: Cryptographic private keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

compliant.description: Loading private key from secure key management service
compliant.code:
import boto3

# Load private key from AWS Secrets Manager
def get_private_key():
    client = boto3.client('secretsmanager')
    response = client.get_secret_value(SecretId='my-private-key')
    return response['SecretString']

compliant.description: Reading private key from secure file
